lenses.guidance: vv 1-16 use covenant-farewell form — review past faithfulness (vv 3-5), warn against compromise (vv 6-13), name covenant consequences (vv 14-16). The structural pattern matches Moses's farewell at Deut 31; Joshua dies in his teacher's voice.

vv 14-16's covenant-blessing-and-curse warnings keep the redemption arc honest — possession of the land does not guarantee permanence. The covenant promise remains conditional in tone; obedience or exile, blessing or curse.

v 8's 'hold fast to the LORD your God' compresses Joshua's life into one imperative. To live faithfully today is to make covenant-allegiance the heart's permanent grip; nothing else holds when the cultures around press in.
